title: cyperus subumbellatus var. thomensis
intro: Cyperus subumbellatus var. thomensis, also known as the Timor sedge, is a rare and endangered species of sedge that is native to East Timor. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cyperus subumbellatus var. thomensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
